message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: My task: add with_counts param to groupby filter in src/jinja2/filters.py. This is the only file I need to touch. What's your feature/files? If you also need filters.py we should coordinate on non-overlapping edits (e.g. different functions) or I can merge your diff in.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: My task: add case_sensitive param to groupby()/sync_do_groupby() in filters.py (touches _GroupTuple, sync_do_groupby, do_groupby only, lines ~1147-1235). If your feature also needs filters.py, tell me which functions/line ranges you touch so we can split non-overlapping regions of the same file, or let me know your files so we stay disjoint.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Acknowledged: we both must touch sync_do_groupby/do_groupby in filters.py since case_sensitive and with_counts are separate new params on the same functions. Proceeding to implement case_sensitive now, keeping the diff minimal (only touching the groupby default/expr/key lines + docstring). Whoever merges last should manually combine both param additions into one final signature (attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False, with_counts=False) rather than letting git silently pick one side. Will send DONE when finished.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Confirmed: we both touch sync_do_groupby/do_groupby/_GroupTuple in filters.py, true overlap, can't be disjoint. Plan: I'll add with_counts=False as a new trailing kwarg (yields (grouper, items, count) tuples when True, via _GroupTuple gaining an optional count field). You add case_sensitive as a trailing kwarg on your side. We each implement independently now; whoever merges second will hand-resolve the conflict so the final signature has both params and _GroupTuple supports both. I'll proceed to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished with_counts implementation. Design: added a separate _GroupCountTuple(grouper, list, count) NamedTuple instead of adding a field to _GroupTuple, so default (with_counts=False) behavior/unpacking is byte-for-byte unchanged (a fixed-arity NamedTuple can't conditionally drop a field per-call). sync_do_groupby/do_groupby gained with_counts: bool = False as the last param, appended after default. All 837 existing tests pass. Committing and pushing now; will merge your branch in before final patch. Let me know your final param order for case_sensitive so I can merge cleanly, or send DONE when ready and I'll resolve it myself.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished case_sensitive param for groupby: added a shared _group_tuples(environment, value, attribute, default, case_sensitive) helper right before sync_do_groupby (around line 1160), and sync_do_groupby/do_groupby now just call it. If your with_counts change also touches sync_do_groupby/do_groupby signatures, when merging please fold case_sensitive into the same signature/helper rather than reverting my change. All local tests pass (837 passed).

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Committed and pushed (e18dfd03). Final signature on my side: sync_do_groupby(environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False). Suggested combined order: (environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False, with_counts=False) so it matches your 'append after default' approach too. I'll fetch team/agent2 now and attempt the merge myself, hand-resolving _group_tuples/_GroupTuple/_GroupCountTuple conflicts to keep both features working together with that param order. Will ping if anything is ambiguous.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Merged team/agent2 into agent1 (commit 183b5a1e), hand-resolved the conflict: final signature is (environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False, with_counts=False) via a combined _group_tuples() helper that both sync_do_groupby and do_groupby call. _GroupCountTuple kept as-is from your implementation. Verified case_sensitive+with_counts compose correctly together, all 837 tests pass. Pushed to team/agent1. patch.txt built from team/main..HEAD contains both features.
